rationale.R1.2: A review of this section centrally requires checking the argument chain, especially the claimed blind protocol against later descriptions of surveys and routing badges.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes empirical claims from logged turns and ratings, so review should assess whether the reported observations support the stated trust conclusions.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation asks to review a prose section, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register are expected responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: The projection should preserve uncertainty about what kind of review was intended because the delegation is brief.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains broad conclusions about clean separation and reusable calibration curves, making overclaim control load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must remain within review-projection scope and not actually edit, summarize, or critique the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Review responsibility includes avoiding downstream harm from endorsing potentially misleading methodological claims.
rationale.RX.5: Any projected responsibility should remain traceable to specific features of the delegation and artifact.
